name: tarka

metadata.name: tarka
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Various things for common use, like a Swiss Army Knife.

documentation:
# tarka

This library is a collection of various utilities to help develop something else, maybe even for you.

modules.name: tarka.logging.patch
documentation:
The builtin logging facility works with global object instances which make them difficult to customize.
Yet this module shall add additional functionality to logging while keeping it completely compatible.

methods.name: tarka.logging.patch.TarkaLoggingPatcher.patch_handler_tracking
documentation: Add a callback hook to the Logger class for handler changes.

methods.name: tarka.logging.patch.TarkaLoggingPatcher.hook_logger_mirror_handlers
documentation:
Copy the handlers to the logger from the source (root logger by default) and keep them in sync.
This is useful when not propagating loggers need to be used, but they shall have the handlers of the root.

methods.name: tarka.logging.patch.TarkaLoggingPatcher.make_logger_shadow_root
documentation:
Makes a logger (that is not root) stop propagating while mirroring the handlers from the root.
This allows setting different levels for each library while all logging to the same handlers.

methods.name: tarka.logging.patch.TarkaLoggingPatcher.copy_logger_handlers
documentation: Copy the handlers to the logger from the source (root logger by default).

methods.name: tarka.logging.patch.TarkaLoggingPatcher.patch_custom_level
documentation: Patch a custom log level to the Logger class.

functions.name: tarka.logging.formatter.create_logging_formatter
documentation: Better default formats and support for overriding the formatting of asctime, which has no API. (datefmt lacks msec)

variables.name: tarka.logging.formatter.LOGGER_FORMAT_MSEC_WITH_DOT
documentation: use decimal dot

functions.name: tarka.logging.bootstrap.init_tarka_logging
documentation: Use this in a top level __init__ module, to ensure the Logger.trace is patched early for use.

functions.name: tarka.logging.bootstrap.setup_basic_logging
documentation:
Convenience function to setup loggers and handlers easily.
    - Setup handlers for the root logger. (because error logging shall be setup globally)
    - Setup levels for the specified loggers.

A typical usage example:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", "-v", action="count", default=0)
    parser.add_argument("--silent", "-s", action="count", default=0)
    parser.add_argument("--log_file", help="path to write log to")
    parser.add_argument("--log_backups", type=int, default=16, help="how many log files to keep when rotating")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    setup_basic_logging(
        root_handlers=[RotatingFileHandlerConfig(args.log_file, backup_count=args.log_backups)],
        logger_levels={__name__: NamedLoggingLevelConfig(args.verbose, args.silent, default=logging.INFO)},
    )

This should be used as early as possible in the application's lifespan.

functions.name: tarka.logging.level.get_logging_levels_with_name
documentation:
It is astonishing that no public API exists to access the level-name mapping. The comments about levels
say the NOTSET pseudo-level is the lower limit for user defined values. Since there should not be many
more serious levels than FATAL, we iterate over all possible levels to see which has a name.
We exclude the NOTSET from the returned collection.

functions.name: tarka.logging.level.calculate_named_logging_level
documentation: Calculate the most appropriate named logging level by verbosity and silence deltas relative to a default.

functions.name: tarka.logging.utility.get_log_file_path
documentation:
Evaluate the selected path and prepare it to be a logging target.
Expands the path if it has the user's home (~).
If the path does not end with a name of '.log' extension, treat it as a target directory and generate a filename.
Create intermediate directories if they do not exist, which may raise an OSError for various reasons.

classes.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager

methods.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager.instance
documentation:
Automatic global singleton instance support for convenience. Any number of LoggerHandlerManager may be
used with any logger, but commonly only one is needed for the root.
Loggers made by libraries for their own use usually won't need this, since those loggers are only
utilized by that codebase most of the time.

methods.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager.clear
documentation: Remove all managed handlers from the logger.

methods.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager.remove
documentation:
Remove a managed handler from the logger by name.
Return the removed logger or None if no logger is tracked by name.

methods.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager.add
documentation:
Add a handler, replacing the previous one with the same name if any.
Returns the previous handler that was replaced, None otherwise.

methods.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager.remove_config
documentation: handler configs and setup convenience

methods.name: tarka.logging.manager.LoggerHandlerManager.remove_stderr_handler
documentation: specific, but generally useful handlers

The logger objects are global. The root logger especially may get handlers from different contexts and there
is no native solution to track them. Using this a library or application can easily add/remove/update their
handlers on any logger safely, without affecting handlers unknown to them.

modules.name: tarka
documentation:
Tarka

variables.name: tarka.utility.time.format.PRETTY_TIMESTAMP
documentation: default global instance for ease of use

classes.name: tarka.utility.time.format.PrettyTimestamp
documentation:
Timestamps that are human readable, concise, constant-width, with no special characters by default.
Timezone information is omitted. The same tz should be used in the same context to avoid ambiguity.

modules.name: tarka.utility
documentation: Tiny things without external dependencies.

functions.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.sqlite_retry
documentation:
Simple retry logic for handling SQLite operational errors when necessary. Keep in mind that using this can and
should be avoided usually, because the builtin busy_timeout handler is set up by default. There are a few
exceptions that do not utilize the busy_handler even if it is set, like the wal_checkpoint pragma.

The wait time can be absolute (to the process by time.perf_counter()) to retry until, or a duration to retry for.
The duration can be expressed by negative values, which will be translated into absolute time when the first error
is raised.

classes.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase.create
documentation: Strict start-stop helper for automatic cleanup.

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase.stop
documentation: The default timeout is zero, we assume the .closed event will be used to wait for cleanup in asyncio.

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._thread
documentation: SQLite connection and request executor thread.

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._run_job
documentation:
This is designed to be used as

    get_xy = partialmethod(AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._run_job, _get_xy_impl)

which means the wrapped function is not yet bound in the expression. The self argument is automatically
passed by the worker thread to make it work.
This will raise AttributeError if the database has been closed.

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._initialise
documentation:
The connection is ready and the database initialization like pragma definitions shall be done here.

By default the db will use WAL journaling and NORMAL sync policy. These supply the highest throughput
with persistence, when the application can choose to ensure durability at any point by issuing a checkpoint.
Additionally the "wal_autocheckpoint" pragma could be tuned depending on the database use-pattern.

The "busy_timeout" pragma does not need to be set here, as the python binding of SQLite3 does that in the
connection, for which it uses the "timeout" argument.

NOTE: Current execution is not inside a transaction!

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._setup
documentation: The connection is ready and the database initialization like schema shall be done.

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._checkpoint
documentation:
Use in WAL mode with NORMAL synchronous operation to ensure writes are synced to storage!
When guaranteed durability is required at a point, this can be used as a post-process callback like:

    mut_xy = partialmethod(AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._run_job, _mut_xy_impl, post_process_fn=_checkpoint)

If there are multiple writer processes the checkpoint api call can easily return SQLITE_BUSY.
If the database is used by a single process in a single instance, such will not happen.

methods.name: tarka.utility.aio_sqlite.AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._checkpoint_retrying
documentation:
To be used similarly to ._checkpoint(), but this will implicitly retry similar to normal operations as
configured initial timeout value.
Use if multiple connections are expected to operate on the DB and one will require explicit checkpoints
at specific places this may work well enough.
Keep in mind though, that if more durability is required, the synchronous pragma could be tuned instead.

Provide a lightweight asyncio compatible, customizable interface to an arbitrary SQLite database in a safe way.
All SQL connection operations are restricted to be executed on the worker thread, guaranteeing serialization
requirements. If more processes would access the database the transaction_mode selector can be used, but job
specific transaction handling would be needed for optimal performance.

Requests shall be implemented like this:

    def _get_all_impl(self):
        return self._con.execute("SELECT x FROM y").fetchall()

    get_all = partialmethod(AbstractAioSQLiteDatabase._run_job, _get_all_impl)

modules.name: tarka.utility.time.utc
documentation:
These are prepared for use in a server environment, where using local time is undesired.
All datetime objects returned are in UTC offset zero.
All timestamps are considered to be in UNIX epoch format.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc._dt_fromtimestamp
documentation: NOTE: The C backend of fromtimestamp on Windows does not handle ranges before the epoch and farther in the
future, so... don't use it. Luckily the datetime facility in python can  bu used instead easily.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_timestamp
documentation:
Current UTC time.
In Python time.time() calls the time C function.
On Linux, macOS, Windows the epoch is 1970-1-1, 00:00 UTC, and is timezone-independent.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_timestamp_delta
documentation: Get the current timestamp with some offset.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_timestamp_from_datetime
documentation:
This function flips the convention of assuming non-aware datetime to be in local time.
On the server side if a remote request does not specify the timezone, assuming our timezone cannot be
more correct than assuming the remote point to at least speak a common language, just badly.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_timestamp_from_uuid
documentation: The timestamp inside a UUID version 1 is not in UNIX epoch format, so we convert it to that.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_datetime
documentation:
Current UTC time.
Equivalent to calling: datetime.now(timezone.utc)

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_datetime_delta
documentation: Get the current timestamp with some offset.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_datetime_from_timestamp
documentation: Convert the UTC timestamp to an UTC datetime.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_datetime_from_uuid
documentation: Convenience wrapper to get the timestamp of an UUID1 as datetime.

functions.name: tarka.utility.time.utc.utc_datetime_from_string
documentation:
Because datetime.strptime() can only handle a single static format, we regexp-process the input and construct
the datetime object ourselves.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.seq.seq_eq
documentation: Usable to check equality of items in a list and tuple. (or any custom sequence object)

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.seq.seq_closest
documentation:
Return the closest numerical value in an ascending sorted sequence.
In case of two values being equally close, choose the smaller.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.seq.seq_closest_index
documentation:
Return the index of the closest numerical value in an ascending sorted sequence.
In case of two values being equally close, choose the earlier.

modules.name: tarka.utility.serialize.deterministic
documentation:
This provides a canonical binary serialization for the main Python primitive types.
The primary use-case is to be able to produce a deterministic checksum/signature of an arbitrary data structure,
but deserialization is implemented as well.
For faster, space-efficient and wide compatibility serialization where deterministic form is not needed, use msgpack.

functions.name: tarka.utility.serialize.deterministic.deterministic_dumps
documentation: Get the canonical binary serialization of the object in whole.

functions.name: tarka.utility.serialize.deterministic.deterministic_hash
documentation: Calculate the canonical hash of the object.

functions.name: tarka.utility.serialize.deterministic.deterministic_transform
documentation: Process the canonical binary serialization stream of the object.

functions.name: tarka.utility.serialize.deterministic.deterministic_loads
documentation:
Restore the object from the canonical binary serialization.
Implemented without recursion.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.traverse.traverse
documentation:
Recursively traverse over an object structure by customizable traversal rules by object type.
Each visited object is yielded and are directly followed by their children if any.
The implementation uses a stack of iterator objects, it has no recursion depth limit.
The objects that yield children are tracked in the traversal path to avoid cycles.

modules.name: tarka.utility.file
documentation: Deprecated import path for compatibility

classes.name: tarka.utility.file.resources.ResourcesFolder
documentation:
A simple and universal utility to access data files in python packages.
The only restriction is for the files to be in a separate folder that is not a python package. (for clarity)

functions.name: tarka.utility.file.name.split_extension
documentation:
Split extension that respects the common, but peculiar exception of multi-level extensions like ".tar.gz".
Since the extension of name is pretty much optional, arbitrary and has no real standard, the only way to deal
with them is to care for each specific case we are interested in. Therefore this solution is never going to be
complete, but at least it is a solution to some.

NOTE: Additional differences to os.path.splitext():
    - input can't be a path, only a filename is correct
    - ending periods will not be split as an extension
    - no support for bytes

One can argue that "there is no actual multi-level extension and .tar.gz is just .gz". There is no pertinent
case when I want to create an archive "foo.tar.gz" and I consider "foo.tar" the name, so the ".tar" must be
part of the extension by exclusion. The fact that this question arises at all, points out the absolute lack of
any guidance on what an extension should actually be. It is and always was a form of metadata that indicates
the type of content the file holds. The most notable system where this convention is kept is Windows, but even
for them, the official docs only state that an extension is "optional" and "separated from the filename by a
period": https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/fileio/naming-a-file

NOTE: Validating the name for use is not in the scope of this function to be usable by the OS or FS.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.iter_unique
documentation: Yield unique(ly hashable) items for the iterables.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.select_eq_cmp_by_args
documentation:
Select the proper comparison lambdas to use for the properties.
Return a lambda for testing equality, compare-not-equal and compare-or-equal.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.iter_merge_zip
documentation:
Two-way merge-zip using the iterator interface.
The iterators must yield their items sorted in in the order specified by `ascending`.
Either of the yielded tuple values may be the sentinel which indicates miss, but not both.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.iter_merge_two
documentation:
Two-way merge using the iterator interface.
The iterators must yield their items sorted in ascending order.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.iter_merge
documentation:
Multi merge using the iterator interface.
The iterators must yield their items sorted in ascending order.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.iter_merge_unique
documentation:
Yield unique items for the iterables by merge-iterating over them.
The iterators must yield their items sorted in ascending order.

functions.name: tarka.utility.algorithm.iter.iter_all_same
documentation: Check if all values equal in the iterables.

classes.name: tarka.utility.file.safe.SafeFile

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.safe.SafeFile.read
documentation: Read the normal path or fall back to the backup.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.safe.SafeFile.write
documentation: Write a new version of the file, manage the backup automatically.

Provides a way to write and read a file on a path safely. Meaning that writing a new version of the file will
never leave it in an undefined state. In other words, either the new file is successfully written or the old
version is retained.

This utility may be necessary on Windows, because the file moving/replacing (MoveFileEx) is not atomic.

Write new versions of a file safely by utilizing a temporary file and keeping a backup.
Read the file and fallback to the backup if necessary.

Not thread-safe!

functions.name: tarka.utility.importing.forward_import_recursively
documentation: This can be utilized to load plugin-like solutions and structures that utilize subclass-hook for example.

classes.name: tarka.utility.importing.IsolatedPackageImport
documentation:
This can be used to import modules on an arbitrary absolute path and have them all under an isolated package if
they are imported with relative paths.

classes.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread

methods.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread.is_alive
documentation: Determine if the thread is running and not terminating.

methods.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread.is_finished
documentation: Determine if the thread has started and is still running.

methods.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread._keep_running
documentation: The thread should poll this periodically to check if it has to terminate.

methods.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread.stop
documentation: Request the thread to stop and wait for it if desired. Returns True if the thread has stopped before returning.

methods.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread._thread_wrapper
documentation: Provides a finish-callback functionality independent of the main thread execution.

methods.name: tarka.utility.thread.AbstractThread._thread
documentation:
Main thread execution must be implemented here.
The args were received either from the __init__() or the start() call.
Simple thread boilerplate or wrapper with support for stopping by request and a callback at exit.

classes.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile.__init__
documentation:
---
param fs_name_encode_length: Customizable name-length calculator for the initial stripping
  round.
param max_name_length: |-
  This is only to optimize the initial truncation loop. Should match the maximum
  file-name length of the current platform.
---

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile._fs_name_encode_unicode_length
documentation:
This is used for platforms that have support for Unicode filename representation on their primary filesystems.
For these the unicode length shall be considered as the filename length as-is.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile._fs_name_encode_utf8_bytes_length
documentation:
On Linux (and possibly other POSIX systems that originate from the 1970s) the filename length limit is measured
as bytes, with a maximum length of 255. This limitation is so low-level in the kernel and all the file-systems
as well, that it will not be enhanced soon.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile.reserve
documentation:
This is a convenience function that should be used if the file is reserved for later, and no operations are
to be done with it right now.
The reserved name is returned which could be different to the input name.
The directory must exist.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile.open_reserve
documentation:
Returns the open file-descriptor to the reserved file and its name, which could be different to the input name.
The directory must exist.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile._resolve
documentation: Can be overridden if directory resolution errors need special care.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile._adjust_name_direct
documentation:
Can be overridden to customize the filename to be created at the direct reserve attempt.
We need to make a the name split to prepare for inserting a random sequence in case of a conflict.

methods.name: tarka.utility.file.reserve.ReserveFile._adjust_name_parts_temp
documentation:
Can be overridden to customize the filename to be created at the temp/random sequence attempts.
The default implementation adds a separator character to the prefix, so the file name stay visually
clear from random sequence. The suffix should start with a separator dot for the extension, so no
change needed there.

Try and reserve a filename as is in by the given path, by creating it safely with EXCL flag. If that fails
then the file name is to be adjusted with a random value to resolve conflicts.

This is a variant of tempfile.mkstemp() that provides a behavioural override interface for adjusting the file-name
and firstly tries to create the file without a random sequence. Additionally if the filename would be too long for
the system, it will sensibly truncate characters.

classes.name: tarka.utility.sentinel.NamedObject
documentation:
Singleton named object generation and storage.
These objects are ideal to be used as special values (sentinels) in algorithms where such are needed.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.alembic.AlembicHelper.run
documentation: Call the alembic CLI.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.alembic.AlembicHelper.get_head_revision
documentation: Get current single head revision.

modules.name: tarka.asqla
documentation: This implements a slightly opinionated reusable async Alembic+SQLAlchemy setup, for well managed database persistence.

functions.name: tarka.utility.utf8.is_utf8_codepoint_start
documentation:
Returns True if the byte-value could be the first byte of an UTF-8 codepoint.
NOTE: Currently not used, more than 4 bytes sequence starts are also accepted. This should not be a problem,
because they do not collide with the encoding of continuation bytes.
\:param b: assumed to be in range(256) like a value of a bytes sequence

functions.name: tarka.utility.utf8.is_utf8_codepoint_continuation
documentation:
Returns True if the byte-value could be a continuation byte of an UTF-8 codepoint.
\:param b: a value of a bytes sequence

functions.name: tarka.utility.utf8.iter_chunk_keep_utf8_codepoints
documentation: Split the input bytes array into maximum-length chunks while respecting utf8 codepoint boundaries.

modules.name: tarka.utility.envvar
documentation: Handy functions to parse environment-variables to basic types.

functions.name: tarka.utility.envvar.parse_bool_envvar
documentation:
Check if an environment variable is set with a "flag" value.

```
param key

Environment variable name to use.

param default

Value to return when the there is no environment variable with  name.

return

bool or 
```

functions.name: tarka.utility.envvar.parse_int_envvar
documentation:
Get an environment variable parsed as an integer.

```
param key

Environment variable name to use.

param default

Value to return when the there is no environment variable with  name.

return

int or 
```

\:raises ValueError when the environment variable's value can't be parsed as an integer.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database._init_engine
tarka.asqla.database.Database.startup
documentation: Initialize engine first, then bootstrap or migrate schema of the database to be up-to-date.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database._pre_upgrade_hook
documentation:
Place to put custom DDL commands before alembic upgrade.
Keep in mind that it is usually better to implement all changes as alembic revisions.
NOTE: This is called before bootstrap (first upgrade) as well.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database._post_upgrade_hook
documentation:
Place to put custom DDL commands after alembic upgrade.
Keep in mind that it is usually better to implement all changes as alembic revisions.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database._start_hook
documentation:
Place to put custom DDL commands that are executed each time a server starts.
The connection is currently at the HEAD alembic revision, in the upgrade transaction.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database.run
documentation: Convenience wrapper for startup & shutdown.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database.serializable_tx
documentation: Get a transaction executor with SERIALIZABLE isolation_level and automatic retry.

methods.name: tarka.asqla.database.Database.session_serializable_tx
documentation: Get an ORM transaction executor with SERIALIZABLE isolation_level and automatic retry.

functions.name: tarka.asqla.postgres.pg_try_advisory_lock
documentation: Acquire advisory session lock, yields False if the lock was not acquired.

functions.name: tarka.asqla.postgres.pg_advisory_lock
documentation: Acquire advisory session lock, block until acquired.
